metadata.website_type: construction piling and earthworks services website

tasks.name: Request a quote for residential screw piling for a 2-storey extension under 8m depth
tasks.description: Find a residential piling service that uses screw piles and is suitable for foundations up to at least 8m deep, then submit a quote request form for a 2-storey house extension in postcode 3000.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation bar, click on the 'Services' menu item.
From the services dropdown or listing, select the 'Piling' category to open the piling services page.
On the piling services page, use the project type filter to select 'Residential' and apply the filter.

Use the pile type or keywords filter to select or search for services that mention 'screw piles', then locate a service whose description states it is suitable for foundations up to at least 8 meters deep.
Click on the title or 'View Details' button of that screw piling service to open its detail page.
On the service detail page, click the 'Get Quote' or 'Request Quote' button to open the quote request form for this service.

In the quote form, enter '2-storey rear house extension' in the project description field, type '3000' in the postcode field, select 'Residential' as project type, choose 'Sandy soil' (or closest option) from the soil type dropdown, select 'Within 3 months' for project start timeframe, and click the 'Submit Quote Request' button.

tasks.name: Estimate pile quantity and cost for a warehouse and request a quote under $60,000
tasks.description: Use the pile quantity/cost calculator to estimate piles for a 30m x 20m warehouse on firm clay and ensure the estimated total cost is under $60,000, then send a quote request using those inputs.
steps: From any page, click on the 'Calculator' or 'Pile Estimator' link in the main navigation.
On the calculator page, click into the building length field and type '30' as the length in meters.
Click into the building width field and type '20' as the width in meters.
Open the soil type dropdown and select 'Firm clay' or the closest matching soil type option.
Open the building use or load type dropdown and select 'Light industrial / warehouse' or the closest available option, then click the 'Calculate' button.
After the calculator displays an estimated pile quantity and total cost, locate the total cost value and confirm that it is shown as less than $60,000 before proceeding to the next step.
Click the 'Request Detailed Quote' or similarly labeled button associated with the calculator results.

In the quote request form, fill in the project name as '30m x 20m warehouse', enter '5000' in the postcode field, select 'Industrial' as project type, type any example email such as 'client@example.com' in the email field, enter a phone number, and click the 'Submit' button to send the quote request based on the calculator inputs.

tasks.name: Book the cheaper piling rig hire for 3 days with at least 4-star rating under $1,000/day
tasks.description: Compare available piling rig hire options, find rigs with at least a 4-star rating and a daily hire rate under $1,000, choose the cheapest of those, and create a 3-day hire booking.
steps: From the homepage, click on the 'Equipment Hire' option in the main navigation.
On the equipment hire page, use the category filter to select 'Piling rigs' or the closest matching category.
If available, open the rating filter and set it to show only equipment with ratings of 4 stars and above.
Use the price filter or slider to set the maximum daily rate to $1,000 and apply the filter.
Sort the filtered rig list by price using the sort dropdown by choosing 'Price: Low to High' or the equivalent option.
From the sorted list, identify the first piling rig that meets the filter criteria (at least 4-star rating and under $1,000 per day) and click its title or 'View Details' to open its detail page.
On the rig detail page, click the 'Add to Booking' or 'Hire Now' button, then in the hire duration field or dropdown, set the hire period to '3 days'.

Proceed to the booking summary or cart page by clicking the 'Review Booking' or 'Go to Booking' button, and on that page fill in required fields such as site postcode '4000' and contact name, then click the 'Confirm Booking Request' button.

tasks.name: Shortlist 3 Melbourne basement excavation projects completed between 2020–2023
tasks.description: Find case studies of basement excavations located in Melbourne, filter them to projects completed between 2020 and 2023, and add exactly 3 of them to a shortlist or comparison list.
steps: Click on the 'Projects' or 'Case Studies' link in the main navigation to open the projects listing page.
On the projects page, open the project type filter and select 'Basement excavation' or the closest matching project type.
Open the location filter and select 'Melbourne' (or 'VIC / Melbourne region' if that is the closest location option), then apply the filters.
Use the date or completion year filter to select projects completed between '2020' and '2023' inclusive, then apply this filter.
Scroll through the filtered project list and identify the first three projects that meet the criteria (basement excavation in Melbourne, completed 2020–2023).
For each of these three projects, click the 'Add to Shortlist', 'Add to Favourites', or 'Compare' button associated with the project card.
After adding all three projects, click the 'View Shortlist' or 'Compare Projects' link or button usually found near the top or bottom of the list.
On the shortlist or comparison page that opens, confirm the presence of three projects by visually identifying their titles, and leave the shortlist open for further review.

tasks.name: Locate safety certifications and send a safety compliance enquiry referencing ISO 45001
tasks.description: Find the company’s safety and quality certifications page, confirm that ISO 45001 is listed among the certifications, and send a message via the contact form asking about safety compliance and referencing ISO 45001 by name.
steps: From any page, scroll to the footer and click on the 'About', 'Certifications', or 'Safety & Quality' link, whichever is available and most relevant.
On the company information or certifications page, scroll until you find the list or section that mentions formal certifications.
Within that list, locate any item that references 'ISO 45001' and note its exact wording for use in your enquiry.
Use the main navigation or a prominent button to navigate to the 'Contact' or 'Contact Us' page.
On the contact page, locate the general enquiry form and click into the 'Subject' or 'Enquiry type' field and type or select 'Safety compliance' as the subject.
In the message or enquiry text area, type a short message that specifically includes the text 'ISO 45001' and asks about the company’s safety management practices.
Fill in the remaining required fields, such as name, email (e.g., 'safety.manager@example.com'), and phone number, using any placeholder data as needed.
Click the 'Send Message' or 'Submit Enquiry' button to submit this safety compliance enquiry referencing ISO 45001.

tasks.name: Find typical duration for a 10-lot subdivision earthworks project and request a callback in the morning
tasks.description: Use FAQs or resources to determine the typical duration for earthworks on a 10-lot subdivision, then request a callback via a form and specify a preferred contact time between 9:00–11:00 AM.
steps: From the homepage, click on the 'Resources', 'Help', or 'FAQ' link in the main navigation or footer.
On the FAQ or resources page, use the search field or category filters to search for 'subdivision earthworks' or 'subdivisions'.
From the search results or category list, click on an FAQ or article that mentions duration or timelines for subdivision earthworks.
Scroll through that FAQ or article until you identify a stated typical duration or range for completing earthworks on multi-lot or 10-lot subdivisions.
Next, navigate to the 'Contact' or 'Request Callback' page via the main navigation or a call-to-action button.
On the callback form, fill in your name and phone number, and in the project description field type '10-lot subdivision earthworks schedule enquiry'.
In the preferred contact time or time-of-day field, select or type a time window that fits between 9:00 AM and 11:00 AM (for example '9:30–10:30 AM').
Click the 'Request Callback' or 'Submit' button to send the callback request with the specified morning contact window.

tasks.name: Schedule a site inspection for retaining wall piling at the nearest branch to postcode 2000 on a specific date
tasks.description: Use the site inspection booking tool to choose the branch closest to postcode 2000, select 'Retaining wall piling' as the service, and book an inspection on the 15th of next month at any available time slot.
steps: From the homepage, click on the 'Book Site Inspection' or similarly labeled button in the header or banner.
On the booking page, locate the postcode or location field and type '2000' as the site postcode.
Use the branch or location dropdown to select the branch that is indicated as 'closest' or 'nearest' to postcode 2000, then proceed to the next step of the booking flow.
In the service type dropdown or selection list, choose 'Retaining wall piling' or the closest matching service type.
Open the date picker control and use the next-month arrow to navigate to the next calendar month, then click on the date labeled '15'.
After selecting the date, choose any available time slot shown for that day by clicking on one of the time options (for example, '10:00 AM').
Fill in the required contact information fields such as name, phone number, and email, and optionally type 'Retaining wall along rear boundary' in the notes or additional details field.
Click the 'Confirm Booking' or 'Submit Inspection Request' button to complete the site inspection scheduling process.

tasks.name: Configure an earthworks package under 40 total machine hours and request a quote under $20,000
tasks.description: Create a custom earthworks package including excavation, haulage, and compaction services, ensuring the total machine hours do not exceed 40 and the estimated cost is under $20,000, then request a quote for that configuration.
steps: From any page, click on the 'Services' menu and select 'Earthworks' from the dropdown or services list.
On the earthworks services page, locate and click the 'Build Your Package', 'Custom Earthworks', or similar configurator button.
Within the configurator, add 'Excavation' to the package and set its hours field to '16' hours.
Next, add 'Haulage' or 'Truck hire' to the same package and set its hours or load-equivalent field to '12' hours.
Add 'Compaction' or 'Roller hire' as a third line item in the package and set its hours field to '8' hours, ensuring the displayed total machine hours in the configurator do not exceed 40.
Review the estimated cost shown for the combined package and, if the total is under $20,000 as displayed, proceed by clicking the 'Request Quote for This Package' button.
In the quote request form that opens, enter 'Custom 3-service earthworks package' in the project name field and '3-lot infill development' in the project description field.

Fill in the remaining required contact details such as name, email (e.g., 'developer@example.com'), phone number, and site postcode '4217', then click the 'Submit Quote Request' button to send the package configuration.

entities.name: ServiceCategory
entities.description: High-level categories for services such as piling, earthworks, retaining walls, and site inspections.

entities.name: Service
entities.description: Individual piling or earthworks services listed on Piling Services and Earthworks Services pages.

fields.name: category_id
fields.description: References ServiceCategory.id

fields.name: project_types
fields.description: Array of project type identifiers, e.g., ['residential','industrial']

fields.name: max_recommended_depth_m
fields.description: Maximum recommended foundation/pile depth in meters

fields.name: suitable_soil_types
fields.description: Array of soil type identifiers the service is suitable for, e.g., ['sandy_soil','firm_clay']

entities.name: EarthworksPackageConfiguration
entities.description: Configured earthworks package composed of multiple machine-hour items with total hours and cost.

fields.name: item_ids
fields.description: Array of EarthworksPackageItem.id values included in this configuration

fields.name: hours_limit
fields.description: Configured hours limit for validation, e.g., 40

fields.name: budget_limit
fields.description: Configured budget limit for validation, e.g., 20000

entities.name: EarthworksPackageItem
entities.description: Line item within a custom earthworks package, such as excavation or haulage and its hours.

fields.name: package_id
fields.description: References EarthworksPackageConfiguration.id

entities.name: PileEstimate
entities.description: Calculated estimate from the Pile Estimator tool, including inputs and estimated quantities and costs.

entities.name: QuoteRequest
entities.description: Quote requests submitted from service details, pile estimator, or earthworks package configurator.

fields.name: related_service_id
fields.description: References Service.id when quote comes from a specific service

fields.name: related_pile_estimate_id
fields.description: References PileEstimate.id when quote is based on a pile estimate

fields.name: related_earthworks_package_id
fields.description: References EarthworksPackageConfiguration.id when quote is for a custom package

fields.name: budget_cap
fields.description: Optional budget cap provided by the enquirer

entities.name: Equipment
entities.description: Hireable equipment such as piling rigs, excavators, trucks, and rollers.

fields.name: daily_rate
fields.description: Daily hire rate

fields.name: average_rating
fields.description: Average star rating, e.g., 4.5

entities.name: HireBooking
entities.description: Booking summary/cart for equipment hire requests including selected items and contact details.

fields.name: item_ids
fields.description: Array of HireBookingItem.id values

entities.name: HireBookingItem
entities.description: Individual equipment line item within a hire booking.

fields.name: booking_id
fields.description: References HireBooking.id

fields.name: equipment_id
fields.description: References Equipment.id

fields.name: equipment_name
fields.description: Snapshot of equipment name at time of booking

entities.name: Project
entities.description: Completed projects and case studies displayed on the Projects page.

fields.name: completion_year
fields.description: Year the project was completed, e.g., 2021

fields.name: gallery_image_urls
fields.description: Array of image URLs

entities.name: ProjectShortlist
entities.description: Shortlist or comparison set of projects selected by the user.

fields.name: project_ids
fields.description: Array of Project.id values added to the shortlist

entities.name: Certification
entities.description: Formal safety, quality, and environmental certifications such as ISO 45001.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Standard code such as 'ISO 45001'

entities.name: ResourceArticle
entities.description: FAQs and resource articles used to provide information such as typical subdivision earthworks durations.

fields.name: topics
fields.description: Array of topic keywords, e.g., ['subdivision earthworks']

fields.name: typical_duration_description
fields.description: Text describing typical durations where applicable, e.g., for 10-lot subdivisions

entities.name: ContactEnquiry
entities.description: Messages submitted via the general contact form, including safety compliance enquiries.

fields.name: related_certification_code
fields.description: e.g., 'ISO 45001' when the message references a certification

entities.name: CallbackRequest
entities.description: Callback requests submitted via the Request Callback section on the contact page.

fields.name: preferred_contact_time_window
fields.description: Free-text time window like '9:30–10:30 AM'

entities.name: Branch
entities.description: Operational branches used to determine the nearest location for site inspection bookings.

entities.name: SiteInspectionBooking
entities.description: Bookings for on-site inspections including nearest branch, service type, and date/time.

fields.name: branch_id
fields.description: References Branch.id selected as nearest

fields.name: inspection_date
fields.description: Date or datetime representing the selected inspection date

fields.name: time_slot
fields.description: Selected time slot label, e.g., '10:00 AM'
